title: PDF Specification Reference for Accessibility Engineering
purpose: Structured knowledge base enabling agents to understand PDF internals for accessibility tagging, font handling, and native content preservation.

fileStructure.title: PDF File Structure
fileStructure.overview: A PDF file is a sequence of 8-bit bytes organized into four sections: header, body, cross-reference table, and trailer. The body contains numbered objects that reference each other by ID.

header.description: First line: %PDF-1.7 (or %PDF-2.0). Declares the PDF version. Followed by a binary comment line (bytes > 127) so transfer programs treat the file as binary.

body.description: Contains indirect objects (numbered N 0 obj ... endobj). Each object has a unique ID (object number + generation number). Objects can be: dictionaries, arrays, streams, numbers, strings, names, booleans, or null.

objectTypes.dictionary: Key-value pairs in << /Key Value >>. The fundamental building block — page dicts, font dicts, catalog.
objectTypes.stream: A dictionary followed by binary data between 'stream' and 'endstream'. Content streams, font programs, images all use this.
objectTypes.array: Ordered list in [ item1 item2 ... ]. Used for page kids, color values, bbox coordinates.
objectTypes.name: Starts with /. Used as dictionary keys and enum values: /Type, /Font, /Page.
objectTypes.string: Text in (parentheses) or hex in <hexbytes>. Used for text content, dates, metadata.
objectTypes.reference: Points to another object: 5 0 R means 'object 5, generation 0'.

xrefTable.description: Maps object numbers to byte offsets in the file. Enables random access without scanning the whole file. Located near the end, pointed to by 'startxref' value.
xrefTable.corruption: When the xref table is damaged, PDF readers must scan the entire file for 'N 0 obj' patterns to rebuild it. Our corruption-repairer does exactly this.
xrefTable.crossReferenceStreams: PDF 1.5+ can use compressed xref streams (/Type /XRef) instead of plain-text tables. More compact but harder to repair manually.

trailer.description: Contains /Root (catalog reference), /Size (total objects), /Info (document info dict), and /ID (file identifiers). The trailer is the entry point for reading the entire document.

documentStructure.title: Document Object Hierarchy
documentStructure.overview: The catalog (/Root) points to the pages tree, metadata, structure tree, AcroForm, and other document-level dictionaries.

catalog.description: Root of the document. Required entries: /Pages. Optional: /StructTreeRoot (tagged PDF), /MarkInfo, /AcroForm, /Metadata, /ViewerPreferences, /Lang.

accessibilityKeys./StructTreeRoot: Root of the logical structure tree (tagged PDF). Required for PDF/UA.
accessibilityKeys./MarkInfo: Dictionary with /Marked true — declares this is a tagged PDF.
accessibilityKeys./Lang: Document language as BCP-47 tag (e.g., 'en-US'). Required for PDF/UA.

pagesTree.description: A balanced tree of /Type /Pages nodes. Leaf nodes are /Type /Page. Each page has /MediaBox (page dimensions), /Resources (fonts, images), and /Contents (content stream references).
pagesTree.coordinateSystem: PDF origin is at the BOTTOM-LEFT of the page. Y increases upward. This is the opposite of screen coordinates where Y increases downward. Our pipeline must flip Y when matching operators to semantic nodes.

resources.description: Each page (or XObject form) has a /Resources dictionary containing sub-dictionaries for /Font, /XObject, /ExtGState, /ColorSpace, /Pattern, /Shading, /Properties.
resources.fontResources: The /Font subdictionary maps symbolic names (like /F1, /TT0) to font dictionaries. Content stream operators reference these names via Tf.

contentStreams.title: Content Stream Operators
contentStreams.overview: Content streams are sequences of operators that draw text, graphics, and images on a page. Operators are postfix: operands come before the operator name. The graphics state is a stack managed by q (save) and Q (restore).

BT.description: Begin Text object. Initializes the text matrix and text line matrix to identity.

ET.description: End Text object. Text operators are only valid between BT and ET.

Tf.description: Set text font and size. 'font' is a name from the page's /Resources/Font dictionary. '/F1 12 Tf' means use font F1 at 12pt.

Td.description: Move to the start of the next line, offset by (tx, ty) from the start of the current line.

Tm.operands: a b c d e f
Tm.description: Set the text matrix directly. [a b c d e f] is a 3x3 transformation matrix. e,f are the translation (position).

Tj.description: Show a text string. The string bytes are decoded through the font's encoding to produce glyph indices.

TJ.description: Show text with individual glyph positioning. Array alternates strings and numeric adjustments: [(Hello) -250 (World)] shifts 'World' left by 250/1000 of a unit.

'.description: Move to next line and show text. Equivalent to T* then Tj.

\".description: Set word and character spacing, move to next line, show text.

T*.description: Move to the start of the next line (uses the current leading value).

graphicsStateOperators.q: Save the current graphics state (push onto stack)
graphicsStateOperators.Q: Restore the most recently saved graphics state (pop from stack)
graphicsStateOperators.cm: Concatenate matrix: a b c d e f cm — modifies the current transformation matrix
graphicsStateOperators.gs: Set graphics state parameters from an ExtGState dictionary

pathOperators.m: Move to point (start new subpath): x y m
pathOperators.l: Line to point: x y l
pathOperators.c: Cubic Bezier curve: x1 y1 x2 y2 x3 y3 c
pathOperators.re: Rectangle: x y width height re
pathOperators.n: End path without filling or stroking (used for clipping)

BMC.description: Begin marked-content sequence. Tag is a name like /Artifact, /Span, /P.

BDC.description: Begin marked-content sequence with properties. '/P <</MCID 0>> BDC' starts a paragraph with marked-content ID 0.

EMC.description: End marked-content sequence. Every BMC/BDC must have a matching EMC.

markedContentOperators.nativeTaggingUsage: For native PDF tagging, we inject BDC/EMC around existing text operators. Each BDC carries an MCID that links the content to a structure tree element. This is how we tag without rasterizing.

renderingModes.3: Neither fill nor stroke (INVISIBLE — used for our text overlay in raster mode)
renderingModes.4-7: Same as 0-3 but also add to clipping path

fontSystem.title: PDF Font Architecture
fontSystem.overview: PDF supports multiple font technologies. Each font has a dictionary in the page's /Resources/Font, a FontDescriptor with metrics, and optionally an embedded font program.

Type1.description: Adobe's original outline font format. Uses PostScript-style outlines. Being phased out in favor of OpenType.
Type1.encoding: WinAnsiEncoding, MacRomanEncoding, or custom Differences array. Each byte maps to a glyph name.
Type1.embedding: Font program stored in /FontFile stream (PFB format).
Type1.standard14: 14 fonts (Helvetica, Times, Courier, Symbol, ZapfDingbats + variants) that viewers are supposed to have built-in. PDF/UA FORBIDS relying on these — they must be embedded.

TrueType.description: Apple/Microsoft outline format. Uses quadratic Bezier curves. Most common web/system font format.
TrueType.encoding: WinAnsiEncoding or Symbolic. 'cmap' table inside the font maps character codes to glyph IDs.
TrueType.embedding: Font program stored in /FontFile2 stream (TTF binary).

Type0.description: Composite font for CID-keyed fonts (CJK, large character sets). A Type0 font references a DescendantFont (CIDFont).
Type0.encoding: Typically Identity-H (horizontal) or Identity-V (vertical). Maps character codes directly to CID values.
Type0.descendantFont: Either CIDFontType0 (CFF outlines) or CIDFontType2 (TrueType outlines). Has its own FontDescriptor.
Type0.cidSystemInfo: Registry (e.g., 'Adobe'), Ordering (e.g., 'Identity'), Supplement (version number). Required for CID fonts.

Type3.description: Fonts defined by PDF content streams (each glyph is a small drawing). Not searchable or accessible. Flagged by our font health checker.
Type3.accessibilityImpact: Type3 fonts have no standard encoding — text extraction and accessibility are severely impacted.

fontDescriptor.description: Required metadata about the font: /FontName, /Flags (serif, symbolic, italic, etc.), /FontBBox, /ItalicAngle, /Ascent, /Descent, /CapHeight, /StemV.
fontDescriptor./CIDSet: Bit-string enumerating which CIDs are present in a subsetted CID font. If present, must be complete (PDF/UA 7.21.4.2). Our writer removes it to avoid incomplete CIDSet failures.

toUnicode.description: A CMap stream that maps character codes to Unicode values. CRITICAL for text extraction and accessibility. Without it, copy-paste produces gibberish.
toUnicode.requirement: PDF/UA requires every font to have a valid ToUnicode CMap OR a well-defined encoding that maps to Unicode.
toUnicode.reconstruction: Our font-embedder reconstructs missing ToUnicode from: TTF cmap table → Type1 Differences + Adobe Glyph List → CID Registry/Ordering → last-resort synthesis.

subsetting.description: Embedding only the glyphs actually used in the document (not the full font). Reduces file size. Subset fonts have a 6-letter prefix: 'AAAAAA+FontName'.
subsetting.pdfuaImplication: Subset fonts must still have valid ToUnicode for all used glyphs. The /CIDSet (if present) must enumerate all CIDs in the subset program, not just the used ones.

taggedPdf.title: Tagged PDF (Logical Structure)
taggedPdf.overview: Tagged PDF adds a logical structure tree to the document, enabling accessibility tools to understand the document's semantic content independently of its visual layout.

structureTree.description: A tree of structure elements rooted at /StructTreeRoot in the catalog. Each element has a /Type (like /P, /H1, /Table, /TD) and references marked content in content streams via MCIDs.

structureElement./S: Structure type name: Document, Part, Sect, H1-H6, P, L, LI, Table, TR, TH, TD, Figure, Formula, Span, Link, etc.
structureElement./K: Kids: array of MCIDs (integers), other structure elements, or marked-content references
structureElement./Pg: Page reference for this element's content
structureElement./A: Attribute objects (like table Scope, RowSpan, ColSpan)

markedContent.description: Content stream operators wrapped in BDC/EMC pairs with an MCID property link the visual content to structure elements.
markedContent.example:
/P <</MCID 0>> BDC
  BT /F1 12 Tf 72 700 Td (Hello World) Tj ET
EMC
markedContent.howItWorks: The MCID (Marked Content ID) is an integer unique within a page. The structure element's /K array contains this MCID, linking the logical structure to the physical content.

parentTree.description: A number tree (/ParentTree) in the structure tree root that maps each page's MCID values to their parent structure elements. Enables fast lookup: given an MCID on page N, find the structure element.
parentTree.requirement: Every marked-content sequence with an MCID must have a corresponding entry in the ParentTree.

tableStructure.requiredElements: Table → TR → TH|TD. THead/TBody/TFoot are optional but recommended grouping elements.

attributes.Scope: On TH cells: 'Row', 'Column', or 'Both'. Declares which data cells this header applies to.
attributes.Headers: On TD cells in complex tables: space-separated list of TH element IDs that define this cell.
attributes.RowSpan: Number of rows this cell spans (default 1).
attributes.ColSpan: Number of columns this cell spans (default 1).

tableStructure.pdfuaRequirement: Every TH cell must have a Scope attribute. Complex tables (multiple header rows/columns) should also use Headers on TD cells.

artifacts.description: Content that is not part of the logical document (headers, footers, page numbers, watermarks, decorative graphics). Wrapped in /Artifact BMC ... EMC.
artifacts.subtypes: Pagination (header, footer, page number), Layout (decorative rules, backgrounds), Page (page-level artifacts)

pdfUA.title: PDF/UA (ISO 14289) — Universal Accessibility
pdfUA.overview: PDF/UA specifies requirements for accessible PDF documents. It mandates tagged structure, proper reading order, alternative text for images, and font embedding.
keyRequirements: All content must be tagged (no untagged content outside Artifact)
Logical reading order must match visual reading order
All fonts must be embedded with valid ToUnicode
Standard 14 fonts must not be used without embedding
Images must have /Alt text or be marked as Artifact
Table structure must use TH with Scope
Document must have /Lang, dc:title in XMP, and PDF/UA identification
Form fields must have accessible names
No content that relies solely on color for meaning

ourCompliance.fontsEmbedded: Our font-embedder ensures every font has an embedded program and valid ToUnicode. Standard 14 fonts are replaced with embedded Noto Sans/Serif/Mono/Symbols.
ourCompliance.structureTree: Tag builder creates a complete structure tree with Document root, Sect, H1-H3, P, Table/TR/TH/TD, L/LI.
ourCompliance.nativeTagging: Native mode preserves original content while injecting BDC/MCID/EMC. Raster mode creates invisible text overlay.
ourCompliance.cidSetFix: We remove /CIDSet from subsetted Type0 fonts to satisfy clause 7.21.4.2 (CIDSet completeness).
ourCompliance.tableScope: Every TH cell is guaranteed a Scope attribute (Column, Row, or Both).

ourPipeline.parserOutput: Uses screen coordinates (Y from top, like pdfjs-dist)
ourPipeline.nativeParser: Uses PDF coordinates (Y from bottom, like PDFBox)
ourPipeline.tagMatcher: Must convert between the two: operatorScreenY = pageHeight - operatorPdfY

glossary.MCID: Marked Content Identifier — integer linking content stream marked-content to structure elements
glossary.BDC: Begin Marked-Content sequence with properties (Dictionary form)
glossary.BMC: Begin Marked-Content sequence (simple form, no properties)
glossary.CID: Character Identifier — numeric code in CIDFonts (large character sets like CJK)
glossary.CMap: Character Map — mapping from character codes to CIDs or Unicode
glossary.ToUnicode: CMap specifically for mapping character codes to Unicode values (text extraction)
glossary.FontDescriptor: Dictionary containing font metrics (ascent, descent, bbox, stemV, etc.)
glossary.xref: Cross-reference table — maps object numbers to byte offsets
glossary.StructTreeRoot: Root of the logical structure tree in a tagged PDF
glossary.ParentTree: Number tree mapping MCIDs to parent structure elements
glossary.Artifact: Content not part of the logical document (headers, footers, decorative elements)
glossary.PDF/UA: ISO 14289 — Universal Accessibility standard for PDF
glossary.PDF/A: ISO 19005 — Archival standard for long-term preservation of PDF
glossary.PDFBox: Apache's Java library for PDF creation and manipulation (used by our writer)
